name: True Garlic Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the garlic puree and butter. This can be done days in advance and refrigerated; bring to room temperature before using.
2. Combine the yeast with 1/2 cup warm water in a large bowl. Stir with a fork or small whisk.
3. Add an additional 2 1/2 cups of water. Add salt.
4. Stir in the flour, 1 cup at a time, beginning with the whole wheat. Use a whisk until the dough becomes stiff, then switch to a wooden spoon.
5. Turn the dough onto a well floured work surface. Knead rhythmically for 10 to 15 minutes, until the dough is smooth, springy, nonsticky, and elastic. Add more flour as you knead if necessary.
6. The dough is ready when you can poke your fingers into it and the indentations spring back.
7. Cover the dough with a cloth and let rest while you wash, dry, and generously butter the bowl.
8. Knead the dough a few more turns, then form it into a ball and place it in the bowl. Turn it to coat with butter. Cover the bowl and put it in a warm, draft-free place until the dough has doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours.
9. When doubled, flour your fist and gently punch down the dough. Knead it a few times and let it rest.
10. Sprinkle 1 large or 2 small baking sheets with a liberal amount of cornmeal. Divide the dough into 3 equal parts.
11. While working with one piece, keep the other two covered.
12. Flour your work surface. With a rolling pin, roll each piece of dough into a rectangle approximately 14-inches long by 7-inches wide.
13. Spread softened garlic butter on the dough.
14. Roll the long edge toward the opposite long edge, as if you were rolling up a rug. Pinch ends closed.
15. Place rolls on the baking sheets.
16. With a sharp knife or razor blade, slash the rolls lightly at 2-inch intervals.
17. Cover with a cloth and place in a warm draft-free place to rise until doubled, about 1/2 hour.
18. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
19. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes with a pan of boiling water on the oven floor. Spray loaves with water several times during the baking process.
20. To test for doneness, rap the loaf with your knuckles. The loaf should sound hollow.
21. Cool on wire racks. The loaves are delicious eaten warm right out of the oven.
